# reality/audio testdata

## `welford-parity-golden.json`

Canonical golden vector for the R80b cross-substrate Welford parity
test suite. The Go canonical implementation in this package generated
this file; sister substrates copy verbatim into their test resources
and compare their outputs against it.

### Cross-substrate parity test consumers

Vendored copies live at:

| Substrate | Path |
|---|---|
| Reality (Go canonical) | `audio/testdata/welford-parity-golden.json` (this file) |
| Folio backend (Go) | `flagships/folio/backend/internal/forge/testdata/welford-parity-golden.json` |
| Folio web (TypeScript) | `flagships/folio/web/src/lib/testdata/welford-parity-golden.json` |
| RubberDuck.Property (C#) | `flagships/rubberduck/tests/RubberDuck.Property.Tests/testdata/welford-parity-golden.json` |
| Pigeonhole / Howler / Dipstick (Kotlin) | `flagships/<name>/mobile/shared/src/commonTest/resources/welford-parity-golden.json` (deferred — needs Android Studio) |

### Format

```json
{
  "description": "...",
  "inputs": [<f64>, <f64>, ...],
  "afterStep": [
    {"n": 1, "mean": <f64>, "m2": <f64>},
    ...
  ]
}
```

`afterStep[i]` is the Welford state after consuming `inputs[i]`. Each
substrate's parity test feeds `inputs` into its Welford in order and
asserts the per-step (N, Mean, M2) tuple matches `afterStep[i]` to
floating-point precision (Mean: 1e-12, M2: 1e-9; tighter tolerances
fail under standard-library `math.Sin` round-off across language
runtimes).

### Regeneration

The golden was generated by the canonical Go Welford implementation
in this package. To regenerate (e.g. after intentionally changing the
input stream length or seed):

```bash
go run scripts/gen-golden-welford.go > audio/testdata/welford-parity-golden.json
```

(The `scripts/` generator is a future addition — for now the file is
hand-checked-in. The `TestWelford_GoldenIsSelfConsistent` test in
`parity_test.go` ensures the canonical is consistent with the golden.)

### Background

R80b is the cross-substrate output-parity R-pattern from Session 55
(L01 conformal Go ↔ C# byte-parity, L13 copula Go ↔ C# byte-parity).
This testdata extends R80b coverage to the Welford convergence
primitive, which now ships in 7 substrates per the 2026-05-01 cohort
intake (see `LimitlessGodfather/reviews/NEW_FLAGSHIPS_COHORT_2026-05-01.md`
§13.F).
